graph.theorem_statement:
Finite-time blow-up from a virial identity for focusing mass-critical NLS.

Let \(u(t,x)\) be a sufficiently regular solution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) with finite variance. Assume:

1. mass is conserved:

\[
\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=\|u_0\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=:M>0,
\]

2. energy is conserved and negative:

\[
E(u(t))=E(u_0)<0,
\]

3. the variance

\[
V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx
\]

satisfies

\[
V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0,
\]

4. for every sufficiently regular \(f\),

\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2
\le
\frac{2}{d}\,\|x f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]

Then there exists \(T<\infty\) such that

\[
\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as } t\uparrow T.
\]

nodes.title: Finite-time blow-up from the virial identity
nodes.informal_statement: There exists \(T<\infty\) such that \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty\) as \(t\uparrow T\).
nodes.informal_proof_text: Use the lower bound \(M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\), obtained from the weighted inequality applied to \(u(t)\), mass conservation \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2}=M\), and \(\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}^2=V(t)\). By the finite-time variance-collapse claim, there is \(T<\infty\) with \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\). Since \(M>0\) is fixed, the inequality can remain true only if \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty\) as \(t\uparrow T\).

nodes.title: Weighted inequality gives a gradient lower bound in terms of variance
nodes.informal_statement:
For every time in the interval of existence,
\[
M^2=\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2
\le
\frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Apply the assumed inequality
\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}
\]
with \(f=u(t)\). Mass conservation gives \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2}=M\), so
\[
M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}.
\]
By definition of the variance,
\[
\|xu(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=V(t),
\]
hence \(\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}=\sqrt{V(t)}\), yielding the claim.

nodes.title: Negative constant virial acceleration forces finite-time collapse of the variance
nodes.informal_statement: There exists a finite time \(T>0\) such that \(V(T)=0\); equivalently, \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Since \(E(u_0)<0\), the virial identity gives
\[
V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0.
\]
Thus \(V\) is a quadratic function with negative leading coefficient: integrating twice,
\[
V(t)=8E(u_0)t^2+at+b
\]
for some constants \(a,b\), and the coefficient of \(t^2\) is negative. Therefore \(V(t)\to -\infty\) as \(t\to +\infty\). On the other hand, by definition,
\[
V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx\ge 0
\]
for every time where the solution exists. Hence \(V\) cannot stay positive for all \(t\ge 0\). By continuity, there is a finite \(T>0\) with \(V(T)=0\), and therefore \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).

nodes.title: Local estimate
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Apply the assumed inequality
\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}
\]
with \(f=u(t)\). Mass conservation gives \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2}=M\), so
\[
M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}.
\]
By definition of the variance,
\[
\|xu(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=V(t),
\]
hence \(\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}=\sqrt{V(t)}\), yielding the claim.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Smaller concrete local consequence extracted because it is more directly formalizable than the broader surrounding node.

formal_artifact.lean_statement: theorem reduced_glassey_local_estimate {d : ℕ} {M xu_norm grad_norm uL2_norm : ℝ} (hweighted : uL2_norm ^ 2 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) / d) * xu_norm * grad_norm) (hmass : uL2_norm = M) : M ^ 2 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) / d) * xu_norm * grad_norm
formal_artifact.lean_code:
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

namespace FormalIslands

theorem reduced_glassey_local_estimate
    {d : ℕ} {M xu_norm grad_norm uL2_norm : ℝ}
    (hweighted : uL2_norm ^ 2 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) / d) * xu_norm * grad_norm)
    (hmass : uL2_norm = M) :
    M ^ 2 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) / d) * xu_norm * grad_norm := by
  simpa [hmass] using hweighted

end FormalIslands

edges.label: depends on
edges.explanation: Parent proof uses a more concrete downstream consequence extracted from its local argument.

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: The blow-up conclusion uses that \(V(t)\to 0\) at some finite time \(T\).

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: This refined local claim depends on the broader supporting node it was carved out from.

review_obligations.text: Check that node 'n0' (Finite-time blow-up from the virial identity) has an informal proof that establishes its informal statement, assuming its child dependencies.
Check that node 'n1' (Weighted inequality gives a gradient lower bound in terms of variance) has an informal proof that establishes its informal statement, assuming its child dependencies.

Check that node 'n2' (Negative constant virial acceleration forces finite-time collapse of the variance) has an informal proof that establishes its informal statement, assuming its child dependencies.

Check that node 'n1_refined_local_claim' (Local estimate) matches the verified Lean statement 'theorem reduced_glassey_local_estimate {d : ℕ} {M xu_norm grad_norm uL2_norm : ℝ} (hweighted : uL2_norm ^ 2 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) / d) * xu_norm * grad_norm) (hmass : uL2_norm = M) : M ^ 2 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) / d) * xu_norm * grad_norm'.
Check that formal child 'n1_refined_local_claim' proves exactly what informal parent 'n0' uses across their dependency boundary.
